GlobalPolicy.module-level-focus: Fast deterministic checks close to the module — pytest -k for targeted tests
GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Checks only merged surfaces touched by the current wave — pytest tests/ -v
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Broader integration and regression confidence — pytest tests/ --cov=app
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log secrets, credentials, tokens, private keys, or raw high-risk payloads
GlobalPolicy.evidence-hierarchy: deterministic assertion > trace assertion > log assertion > semantic evaluation
GlobalPolicy.failure-policy: If verification fails, stop and file a failure packet. Do not silently skip or move to next module.
GlobalPolicy.governance-rule: ALL development is GRACE-governed. No module is implemented without a contract. No module is committed without verification. No verification result is implied — it is either run or explicitly blocked.

verification-philosophy.principle: Routing decisions must be explainable, deterministic and safe to replay from logs.
Domain-aware routing is not verified unless domain rules, DNS binding and host-level runtime sync are checked together.
If host tools or dependencies are unavailable, the report must state the exact blocked command and missing prerequisite.
Manual operator-issued VPN configs are not verified unless they are generated through the same backend provisioning and config-rendering path as the working web flow.
Per-plan device limits are not trusted unless device creation, revoke and rotate operations are verified against explicit active-device counts.
Anti-sharing enforcement must separate deterministic device-limit checks from probabilistic handshake anomaly signals and should default to detect-first evidence before auto-blocking.

critical-flows.CF-001: register -> trial subscription -> vpn config delivery -> domain-aware route resolution
critical-flows.CF-002: login -> protected API access -> known RU domain stays local or RU-routed by policy
critical-flows.CF-003: foreign domain with changing CDN IPs -> DNS rebinding -> DE policy remains effective
critical-flows.CF-004: admin updates routing rule -> runtime sync applies changes without breaking unrelated routes
critical-flows.CF-005: unknown domain -> CIDR fallback -> RU baseline or default path remains backward compatible
critical-flows.CF-006: manual internal client command -> complimentary access grant -> vpn client provisioning -> working config export
critical-flows.CF-007: repeat manual internal client command -> same identity reuse or explicit reprovision -> no silent duplicate active client
critical-flows.CF-008: user with active subscription -> create device -> plan device limit enforced before peer provisioning
critical-flows.CF-009: user revokes one device -> slot is freed -> user creates a new device -> new peer works and old peer stops
critical-flows.CF-010: user rotates a compromised device config -> same logical device remains -> old peer stops -> new peer works
critical-flows.CF-011: same peer reused from suspiciously different endpoints -> anomaly event recorded -> admin can block only that device
critical-flows.CF-012: blocked device peer stays unusable while unrelated devices under the same user remain functional

scenario.success: Exact-domain rule wins over wildcard, CIDR and baseline.
scenario.failure: Resolver returns weaker precedence branch when exact rule exists.

scenario.success: Wildcard-domain rule wins when exact rule is absent.
scenario.failure: Resolver skips wildcard and falls into CIDR or baseline unexpectedly.

scenario.success: DNS binding refresh updates active IP set before stale TTL entries leak indefinitely.
scenario.failure: Expired binding remains active or new binding is not propagated.

scenario.success: Runtime sync applies only affected route-set changes for a single rule mutation.
scenario.failure: Unrelated route sets are flushed or stale entries remain.

scenario.success: Admin can inspect policy state and decision reasons, non-admin cannot mutate policy.
scenario.failure: Missing auth enforcement or missing reason field in response.
scenario.required-trace: ROUTE_DECISION_DOMAIN_EXACT plus response-shape and auth assertions in API tests

scenario.success: VPN provisioning still produces config consistent with selected topology and routing policy expectations.
scenario.failure: Route-aware provisioning diverges from effective runtime policy.

scenario.success: Manual internal access grant creates or reuses a clearly marked non-billable access record without invoking payment-provider logic.
scenario.failure: Internal access is missing, duplicated, or silently mixed with normal paid subscription state.

scenario.success: Operator CLI issues a working config through backend provisioning and config rendering, and repeated issuance stays idempotent unless reprovision is explicit.
scenario.failure: CLI falls back to raw host-only config generation, or duplicate active client state is created for the same internal identity.

scenario.success: Device creation is rejected when active device count has reached the effective plan limit.
scenario.failure: A new peer is provisioned despite the plan limit, or the system counts revoked or blocked devices incorrectly.

scenario.success: Revoking a device disables only that device peer and allows a new device to be created in the freed slot.
scenario.failure: Old peer remains active, slot is not freed, or another unrelated device is affected.

scenario.success: Rotating a config keeps the same logical device but replaces its peer and invalidates the old config.
scenario.failure: Rotate creates a hidden second device or leaves the old peer usable.

scenario.success: Suspicious endpoint churn or near-concurrent peer reuse produces a stable anomaly event and raises device suspicion without immediately corrupting unrelated device state.
scenario.failure: Suspicious reuse leaves no structured evidence, or the wrong device is blamed.

scenario.success: Admin can block or unblock a specific device peer and non-admin users cannot invoke the same enforcement surface.
scenario.failure: Enforcement bypasses auth or disables the whole user account instead of the targeted device.

scenario.success: Every device lifecycle or anomaly transition produces an audit record with device_id, user_id and event type.
scenario.failure: Security-relevant actions occur without a durable audit trail.

scenario.success: App refuses to start with default secret_key (ValueError).
scenario.failure: App starts with default key, only emits a warning.

scenario.success: App refuses to start without DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY (RuntimeError).
scenario.failure: Fernet key is derived from JWT secret, creating single point of compromise.

scenario.success: Admin endpoint returns 401 without token after fixing auth bypass.
scenario.failure: Admin endpoint accessible without authentication.

scenario.success: Blacklisted token is rejected by verify_token_with_blacklist.
scenario.failure: Blacklisted token remains valid for authentication.

scenario.success: alembic upgrade head applies all migrations on clean database.
scenario.failure: Migration fails or produces schema inconsistent with models.

scenario.success: Double deploy preserves existing WG keys and client connections.
scenario.failure: Re-deploy regenerates keys, breaking all client connections.

scenario.success: deploy/rollback.sh creates backup and can restore from it.
scenario.failure: Rollback fails or restores corrupted state.

scenario.success: Every /api/v1/admin/* request produces an AdminAuditEvent record.
scenario.failure: Admin requests occur without audit trail.

scenario.success: Frontend builds with zero any usages in TypeScript source.
scenario.failure: tsc --noEmit reports type errors or any usages remain.

scenario.success: All datetime comparisons use timezone-aware values (datetime.now(timezone.utc)).
scenario.failure: datetime.utcnow() still present in source code.

scenario.success: Subscription expiry task decrements server.current_clients counter.
scenario.failure: Counter drifts upward, reporting more clients than exist.

V-M-001.checks: Backend config and DB initialization remain runnable; auth and crypto helpers still load. Default secret_key is rejected with ValueError. DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY is required. Token blacklist functions work with Redis and degrade gracefully without it. All datetime values are timezone-aware.

V-M-002.checks: Identity creation stays deterministic for both normal and internal users, and manual internal-user resolution cannot create duplicate user rows for the same logical client.

V-M-003.checks: Provisioning and config generation remain compatible with route-aware topology, including configs exported for operator-managed internal clients.

V-M-004.checks: Payment and webhook flows remain isolated from routing migration, complimentary internal access can be granted without entering payment-provider logic, and plan device_limit remains visible and mutable through the admin API.

V-M-005.checks: Referral logic remains excluded from manual internal client issuance unless explicitly requested by future policy. Referral code generation is unique, self-referral is rejected, first payment bonus works correctly, and stats aggregation is accurate.

V-M-006.checks: Admin routing inspection and analytics endpoints enforce admin auth and reflect current policy state.
V-M-006.anchors: none-required-for-current-scope; current evidence is admin auth plus response-shape verification in route and stats API tests

V-M-007.checks: Current RU ipset update, custom route sync and tunnel inspection remain safe under host-level assumptions.
V-M-007.tests: backend/tests/test_routing_policy_compat.py plus static review and targeted runtime tests when host tools are available

V-M-008.checks: Background jobs do not auto-assign complimentary internal access or mutate manually managed clients unexpectedly. Subscription expiry task correctly deactivates expired subscriptions and decrements server.current_clients counter. Daily cleanup and handshake anomaly detection tasks run without errors.

V-M-009.checks: User frontend still builds and has no contract mismatch with auth, config and subscription APIs.

V-M-010.checks: Admin frontend builds and reflects new route policy surfaces without auth regression.

V-M-011.checks: Telegram bot remains on the normal user flow and does not silently consume internal-only manual provisioning paths.
V-M-011.tests: backend/tests/test_telegram_auth.py plus static review of telegram-bot/bot.py call surfaces

V-M-012.checks: Deploy and nginx changes do not introduce weak defaults or unsafe routing assumptions, deploy/create-client.sh becomes a thin wrapper over backend CLI instead of generating raw host-only configs, and fresh RU deploys install a host-level awg0 sync watcher so backend-written peers become active on the live interface.
V-M-012.commands: bash -n deploy/deploy-ru-server.sh && bash -n deploy/deploy-on-server.sh && bash -n deploy/deploy-all.sh && bash -n deploy/create-client.sh && bash -n install.sh
V-M-012.evidence: static review of secrets, ports, mounts, backend CLI invocation, host tool expectations and awg0 watcher install path; live RU evidence should include HTTPS reachability, backend health, live tunnel handshake and peer appearance in awg show after both manual and web provisioning

V-M-013.checks: Exact-domain rules override wildcard rules, wildcard rules override CIDR, CIDR overrides RU baseline, and fallback remains deterministic when no rule matches.
V-M-013.anchors: ROUTE_DECISION_DOMAIN_EXACT ROUTE_DECISION_DOMAIN_WILDCARD ROUTE_DECISION_DNS_BOUND_IP ROUTE_DECISION_CIDR ROUTE_DECISION_RU_BASELINE ROUTE_DECISION_FALLBACK

V-M-014.checks: Rule persistence preserves priority, activation state, domain normalization and duplicate handling.
V-M-014.anchors: none-required-for-current-scope; current evidence is persistence, normalization and duplicate-handling behavior in store tests

V-M-015.checks: DNS bindings are refreshed by TTL, expired safely and conflict resolution is explicit.

V-M-016.checks: Admin CRUD and inspection endpoints expose policy state safely and return decision reasons.
V-M-016.anchors: ROUTE_DECISION_DOMAIN_EXACT; current API evidence also relies on response-shape assertions for list, mutation and explain endpoints

V-M-017.checks: Runtime sync applies incremental updates correctly, removes stale bindings and avoids rule leakage between RU and DE sets.

V-M-018.checks: Complimentary internal access is created or reused deterministically, remains visible in normal subscription state and does not invoke payment-provider side effects.

V-M-019.checks: Manual CLI issuance resolves the internal identity, provisions through VPNService, exports a working config and stays idempotent unless reprovision is explicitly requested.

V-M-020.checks: Device records preserve active, revoked, blocked and replaced lifecycle state; migrated legacy clients become deterministic primary devices without losing peer linkage.

V-M-021.checks: Effective plan device limits are enforced before provisioning, revoked devices free slots, blocked devices do not silently bypass policy, and rotate preserves logical device identity.

V-M-022.checks: User-facing device APIs expose only the caller’s devices, allow deterministic create or revoke or rotate flows, and keep one peer per logical device.

V-M-023.checks: Handshake observation updates device last-seen state, flags suspicious endpoint churn or near-concurrent reuse, persists timestamps safely to production Postgres, and does not leak secrets or raw private peer material into logs.

V-M-024.checks: Admin endpoints can list devices, view anomaly state and block or unblock or revoke a specific device peer while preserving user-level auth boundaries.

V-M-025.checks: Every device lifecycle and anomaly escalation writes an audit entry with stable event types and enough context for later investigation.

V-M-026.checks: Admin audit middleware logs all /api/v1/admin/* requests without leaking tokens or sensitive bodies. AdminAuditEvent records persist with admin_id, action, resource_type, resource_id.
V-M-026.tests: static review plus targeted middleware tests when admin audit test suite is added

V-M-027.checks: Token blacklist correctly rejects revoked tokens. Graceful degradation when Redis is unavailable (auth still works). Blacklist TTL matches token expiry.

V-M-028.checks: Alembic env.py correctly imports all models via import_all_models(). Initial baseline migration captures all existing tables. alembic upgrade head works on clean database.

routing-policy-change.required: Run or design targeted tests for V-M-013 through V-M-017.
Review affected critical flow from CF-003 to CF-005.
Confirm stable decision reason logs remain present.

vpn-or-routing-runtime-change.required: High-scrutiny static review of host-level tools, assumptions and rollback path.
Explain exactly which host-bound command could or could not be executed.

admin-surface-change.required: Verify admin auth, endpoint behavior and admin frontend compatibility.

manual-client-provisioning-change.required: Verify CF-006 and CF-007 with backend CLI plus deploy/create-client.sh wrapper coverage.
Confirm operator-created configs are rendered by VPNService rather than raw shell-generated templates.
Confirm complimentary access remains distinguishable from paid subscriptions in state and reporting.
Confirm host-level awg0 peer activation happens automatically after backend writes peer state, both for manual issuance and for web registration paths.

device-access-change.required: Verify CF-008 through CF-012 with deterministic device counts before enabling autonomous execution on device-aware provisioning.
Confirm device-limit checks run before peer creation and before address allocation.
Confirm revoke, rotate and block flows leave an audit record and do not accidentally affect unrelated devices for the same user.
Keep anomaly detection in detect-first mode until phase-level evidence shows false positives are bounded.

telemetry-requirements.format: Important routing logs should use stable machine-readable markers in the form [Routing][resolver][MARKER] or equivalent structured fields.
telemetry-requirements.manual-provisioning-format: Manual provisioning logs should use stable markers in the form [VPN][manual][MARKER] or [Billing][internal][MARKER].
telemetry-requirements.device-format: Device and anti-sharing logs should use stable markers in the form [VPN][device][MARKER] or [VPN][handshake][MARKER].
telemetry-requirements.redaction: Do not log secrets, private keys, raw tokens, payment credentials or full sensitive payloads.
telemetry-requirements.required-fields: decision_reason rule_id domain normalized_domain resolved_ip route_target source ttl_seconds sync_mode
telemetry-requirements.manual-required-fields: internal_identity user_id access_mode complimentary subscription_id client_id route_id entry_node_id output_path reprovision
telemetry-requirements.device-required-fields: user_id device_id device_name device_status config_version peer_public_key last_endpoint suspicion_score event_type enforcement_action plan_id device_limit
telemetry-requirements.ordering: For trace assertions, event order matters more than prose wording. Prefer stable markers and fields over free text.

verification-levels.module-level: Resolver precedence tests, domain normalization tests, admin auth checks, internal-access policy tests, CLI parity tests, device-limit policy tests and narrow runtime sync tests.
verification-levels.wave-level: Merged checks across routing, vpn and admin surfaces for domain policy mutation, device-bound provisioning and admin enforcement.
verification-levels.phase-level: Full routing migration confidence gate plus device-limit and anti-sharing confidence gate including rollback reasoning, host-tool assumptions, deploy impact review, manual-client provisioning parity review and staged enforcement review.

Phase-1-Gate.required: Resolver precedence tests defined and passing or explicitly blocked with reason.
Backward-compatible fallback to current RU baseline preserved.

Phase-2-Gate.required: DNS TTL handling and runtime sync safety scenarios covered.
No silent rule leakage between dynamic domain bindings and static CIDR sets.

Phase-3-Gate.required: Admin observability endpoints and UI surfaces reviewed for auth and data correctness.

Phase-4-Gate.required: Production rollout review includes rollback, host-tool compatibility and secrets-safe deploy impact.

Phase-5-Gate.required: Operator-issued configs are generated through backend provisioning and connect with the same topology semantics as web-issued configs.
Repeated manual issuance does not create silent duplicate active clients.
Complimentary internal access stays explicit in state and does not invoke payment-provider logic.
Backend-driven peer changes become active on live awg0 without manual operator sync steps.

Phase-6-Gate.required: Legacy single-client users are migrated to deterministic primary devices without losing active access.
Plan device limits are persisted and enforced before any new peer provisioning.

Phase-7-Gate.required: User device creation, revoke and rotate flows pass deterministic module-level checks and preserve one peer per logical device.
Revoking a device frees a slot; rotating a config does not silently create a second active device.

Phase-8-Gate.required: Suspicious handshake reuse leaves structured evidence, admin can review and enforce on a specific device peer, and automatic blocking remains either explicitly disabled or supported by bounded false-positive evidence.
Blocked devices do not quietly regain access through stale peers or freed slots.

Phase-9-Gate.required: App refuses to start with default secret_key (ValueError).
App refuses to start without DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY (RuntimeError).
Admin endpoints enforce authentication (no = None bypasses).
Zero sshpass, base64-encoded passwords, hardcoded IPs, or plaintext credential files in deploy scripts.
Port 8000 not exposed externally in docker-compose.

Phase-10-Gate.required: Zero datetime.utcnow() calls remain in source code.
Alembic baseline migration generated and alembic upgrade head works.
Token blacklist functions exist and degrade gracefully without Redis.
_reprovision_client removes old WG peer after adding new one.
server.current_clients counter decremented on subscription expiry.
All files in docs/ under 500 lines.

Phase-11-Gate.required: httpOnly secure cookies set on auth endpoints.
Zero any usages in frontend-admin TypeScript source.

Phase-12-Gate.required: Double deploy preserves existing WG keys.
Rollback script creates backup and can restore.
All 6 services have healthchecks in docker-compose.
Zero hardcoded eth0 references in deploy scripts.
Nginx serves all security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options).
UFW FORWARD policy is deny with explicit AWG exceptions.

Phase-13-Gate.required: GitHub Actions CI pipeline defined and valid YAML.
CF-012 (blocked device) and CF-007 (CLI idempotency) tests exist and pass.

Phase-14-Gate.required: UserResponse.from_user() factory used in all router endpoints.
AdminAuditEvent model exists and middleware registered.
No dead App.tsx in admin frontend.

agent-reporting.rule: Always list which commands were actually executed and which were only planned.
When tests are still planned but not implemented, say that explicitly instead of implying coverage exists.
Every routing-critical report must name the decision reason branch that was affected.

Gate-Phase-1-8.goal: All implementation phases completed with passing module-local and wave-level verification

required-evidence.evidence-1: All 25 modules (M-001 — M-025) have verification entries (V-M-001 — V-M-025)
required-evidence.evidence-2: Device lifecycle tests cover create, limit-check, revoke, rotate, block, unblock
required-evidence.evidence-3: Handshake anomaly detector emits structured events with suspicion scores
required-evidence.evidence-4: CLI idempotency verified — no duplicate active clients for same identity

Gate-Phase-9-14.goal: All 6 hardening phases delivered with passing security, stability, quality, infrastructure, CI, and cleanup checks

commands.command-2: cd backend && ruff check app/ tests/

required-evidence.evidence-2: CI pipeline passes lint + test + build on every push
required-evidence.evidence-3: Token blacklist functional with Redis, graceful degradation tested
required-evidence.evidence-4: Alembic baseline migration generated and coexists safely with SQLModel.create_all
required-evidence.evidence-5: No security regressions in auth, billing webhook, or admin endpoints

FailurePacket.expected: What should have happened per contract or scenario
FailurePacket.first-divergent-block: START_BLOCK name where execution first deviated
FailurePacket.root-cause-hypothesis: Why the divergence occurred (bug, missing dependency, env issue)
FailurePacket.next-action: Smallest fix to test first, then broader fix if hypothesis confirmed

escalation-rules.rule: If the same scenario fails 3 times with different root causes, escalate to $grace-fix for architectural review
If a failure is caused by missing environment (host tools, DB, Redis), mark as ENV_BLOCKED, not TEST_FAILED
If a failure is caused by stale GRACE doc state, run $grace-refresh before attempting fix
